response.justification: Wei Chen is a capable full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with a long career, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise as its primary technical pillar — and that is largely absent here. C++ appears in only a single 6-month role at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; it is not even listed among the candidate's self-reported skills. The rest of the career is firmly anchored in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. Additionally, the role's core context — industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, MQTT, and industrial protocols — has no meaningful representation in the resume. The candidate does not meet the foundational technical requirements for this position.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems architecture experience across multiple roles (Dunnhumby microservices, GISCollective platform)
Node.js and REST API expertise aligns with the role's advantageous skills
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months in 2014–2015) and is absent from the candidate's own skills list — a critical gap for a role whose title and core responsibilities are C++-centric
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded-systems domain experience; the entire career is web, mobile, and SaaS
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any industrial cybersecurity framework, which are explicit role requirements
